title.raw: 卷八 孝靈帝紀 Volume 8: Annals of Emperor Xiaoling

sentences.zh: 孝靈皇帝諱宏，肅宗玄孫也。

translations.literal: Emperor Xiaoling’s taboo-name was Hong; he was the great-great-grandson of Emperor Zhang (Suzong).
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Xiaoling’s personal name was Hong; he was a fourth-generation descendant of Emperor Zhang.

sentences.zh: 曾祖河間孝王開，祖淑，父萇。

translations.literal: His great-grandfather was Prince Xiao of Hejian, Kai; his grandfather was Shu; his father was Chang.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandfather was Kai, posthumously titled Prince Xiao of Hejian; his grandfather Shu; his father Chang.

sentences.zh: 世封解瀆亭侯，帝襲侯爵。

translations.literal: There being hereditary enfeoffment as Marquis of Jiedu pavilion, the emperor inherited the marquisate.
translations.idiomatic: The line had long held the title Marquis of Jiedu pavilion, and he succeeded to that marquisate before ascending the throne.

sentences.zh: 母董夫人。

translations.literal: His mother was Lady Dong.
translations.idiomatic: His mother was Lady Dong.

sentences.zh: 桓帝崩，無子，皇太后與父城門校尉竇武定策禁中，使守光祿大夫劉儵持節，將左右羽林至河間奉迎。

translations.literal: Emperor Huan died without a son; the empress dowager and her father, Commandant of the Gates Dou Wu, fixed the succession within the forbidden precincts, ordered Acting Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Liu Su to bear the credentials of office, and led the left and right regiments of the Feathered Forest to Hejian to welcome him.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Huan died heirless, the empress dowager and her father Dou Wu, commandant of the Gates, chose the next ruler inside the palace; they sent Acting Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Liu Su with the imperial credentials to lead the Feathered Forest guards to Hejian and escort the new emperor to the capital.

sentences.zh: 使護羌校尉段颎討先零羌。

translations.literal: He ordered Colonel Protecting the Qiang Duan Jiong to campaign against the Xianling Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Jiong, colonel protecting the Qiang, was ordered to attack the Xianling Qiang.

sentences.zh: 二月辛酉，葬孝桓皇帝於宣陵，廟曰威宗。

translations.literal: On xinyou of the second month, Emperor Xiaohuan was buried at Xuan Mausoleum; his temple name was Weizong.
translations.idiomatic: On the day xinyou in the second month, Emperor Huan was interred at Xuan Mausoleum; his temple designation was Weizong.

sentences.zh: 庚午，謁高廟。

translations.literal: On gengwu, he visited the shrine to the High Ancestor.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu he paid his respects at the shrine of the High Ancestor.

sentences.zh: 辛未，謁世祖廟。

translations.literal: On xinwei, he visited the shrine to Shizu.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei he visited the temple of Emperor Guangwu, the founding emperor.

sentences.zh: 大赦天下。

translations.literal: He issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty was proclaimed throughout the realm.

sentences.zh: 賜民爵及帛各有差。

translations.literal: He bestowed noble ranks and silks on the common people, each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: Commoners received noble ranks and silk gifts in graded amounts.

sentences.zh: 段颎大破先零羌於逢義山。

translations.literal: Duan Jiong greatly defeated the Xianling Qiang at Fengyi Mountain.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Jiong inflicted a crushing defeat on the Xianling Qiang at Mount Fengyi.

sentences.zh: 五月丁未朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On dingwei, new moon of the fifth month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The new moon on dingwei in the fifth month was marked by a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 詔公卿以下各上封事，及郡國守、相舉有道之士各一人；

translations.literal: He ordered the ministers of state and below each to submit sealed memorials, and the commandery and kingdom governors and chancellors each to recommend one man of the Way;
translations.idiomatic: An edict called on ministers down the ladder to file sealed memorials and required each governor or chancellor to nominate one candidate distinguished for moral insight.

sentences.zh: 又故刺史、二千石清高有遺惠、為眾所歸者，皆詣公車。

translations.literal: Furthermore, former regional inspectors and two-thousand-shi officials who were pure and elevated, had left benevolent legacies, and were whom the multitude turned to—all were to present themselves at the Palace Coaches Office.
translations.idiomatic: Former inspectors and former two-thousand-shi officials known for integrity, lasting kindness, and popular esteem were likewise summoned to report to the Palace Coaches reception office.

sentences.zh: 太中大夫劉矩為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Counselor Liu Ju was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ju, grand counselor, was appointed grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 六月，京師雨水。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, rain fell in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Heavy rain soaked the capital in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，破羌將軍段颎復破先零羌於涇陽。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, General Who Breaks the Qiang Duan Jiong again defeated the Xianling Qiang at Jingyang.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, in the seventh month, Duan Jiong, general who breaks the Qiang, again routed the Xianling Qiang at Jingyang.

sentences.zh: 八月，司空王暢免，宗正劉寵為司空。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, Minister of Works Wang Chang was dismissed, and Minister of the Imperial Clan Liu Chong was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Chang was removed as minister of works; Liu Chong, minister of the imperial clan, took his place.

sentences.zh: 九月辛亥，中常侍曹節矯詔誅太傅陳蕃、大將軍竇武及尚書令尹勛、侍中劉瑜、屯騎校尉馮述，皆夷其族。

translations.literal: On xinhai of the ninth month, Household Regular Attendant Cao Jiong forged an edict and executed Grand Tutor Chen Fan, General-in-Chief Dou Wu, Director of the Secretariat Yin Xun, Palace Attendant Liu Yu, Colonel of the Garrison Cavalry Feng Shu, and exterminated their whole clans.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the ninth month, the eunuch Cao Jiong forged an edict and had Grand Tutor Chen Fan, General-in-Chief Dou Wu, Director of the Secretariat Yin Xun, Palace Attendant Liu Yu, and Colonel of the Garrison Cavalry Feng Shu put to death; their entire clans were wiped out.

sentences.zh: 皇太后遷於南宮。

translations.literal: The empress dowager was moved to the Southern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: The empress dowager was confined to the Southern Palace.

sentences.zh: 司徒胡廣為太傅，隸尚書事。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Hu Guang was made Grand Tutor and was subordinate to the business of the Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Guang, minister of education, became grand tutor and oversaw the affairs of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 司空劉寵為司徒，大鴻臚許栩為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Liu Chong was made Minister of Education, Grand Herald Xu Xu was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Chong moved from minister of works to minister of education; Grand Herald Xu Xu succeeded him as minister of works.

sentences.zh: 冬十月甲辰晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On jiachen, last day of the tenth month in winter, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse fell on the last day of the tenth month, jiachen.

sentences.zh: 令天下繫囚罪未決入縑贖，各有差。

translations.literal: He ordered that prisoners in the empire whose guilt had not yet been decided might enter silk brocade to redeem themselves, each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: Prisoners whose cases were still undecided were allowed to commute sentences by paying fines in silk, the tariff varying by offense.

sentences.zh: 十一月，太尉劉矩免，太僕沛國聞人襲為太尉。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Grand Commandant Liu Ju was dismissed, and Grand Coachman Wenren Xi of Pei was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Liu Ju was dismissed as grand commandant; Wenren Xi of Pei, grand coachman, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 十二月，鮮卑及濊貊寇幽、幷二州。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the Xianbei and the Huimo raided the two provinces of You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Xianbei and Huimo forces raided You and Bing provinces.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月丁丑，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, first month dingchou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of the reign, on dingchou in the first month of spring, a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 三月乙巳，尊慎園董貴人為孝仁皇后。

translations.literal: On yisi of the third month, the Honored Lady Dong of the Shou Garden was honored as Empress Dowager Xiaoren.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi in the third month the emperor’s mother, Honored Lady Dong of the Shou Garden estate, was elevated with the title Empress Dowager Xiaoren.

sentences.zh: 夏四月癸巳，大風，雨雹。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month guisi, great wind; hail fell as rain.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of summer a violent storm brought hail.

sentences.zh: 詔公卿以下各上封事。

translations.literal: He ordered the ministers of state and below each to submit sealed memorials.
translations.idiomatic: Officials from the highest ministers downward were ordered to submit sealed memorials.

sentences.zh: 五月，大尉聞人襲罷，司空許栩免。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, Grand Commandant Wenren Xi was removed, Minister of Works Xu Xu was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Wenren Xi left the post of grand commandant and Xu Xu was dismissed as minister of works.

sentences.zh: 六月，司徒劉寵為太尉，太常許訓為司徒，太僕長沙劉囂為司空。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, Minister of Education Liu Chong was made Grand Commandant, Minister of Ceremonies Xu Xun was made Minister of Education, Grand Coachman Liu Ao of Changsha was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Liu Chong became grand commandant, Xu Xun minister of education, and Liu Ao of Changsha, grand coachman, minister of works.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，破羌將軍段颎大破先零羌於射虎塞外谷。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, General Who Breaks the Qiang Duan Jiong greatly defeated the Xianling Qiang in the ravine outside Tiger-shooting Pass.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Duan Jiong, general who breaks the Qiang, shattered the Xianling Qiang in the gorge beyond Tiger-shooting Pass.

sentences.zh: 東羌悉平。

translations.literal: The Eastern Qiang were entirely pacified.
translations.idiomatic: The eastern Qiang were finally subdued.

sentences.zh: 九月，江夏蠻叛，州郡討平之。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, the Jiangxia Man rebelled; the province and commanderies campaigned and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month the Man of Jiangxia rose in revolt; provincial and local forces suppressed them.

sentences.zh: 丹陽山越賊圍太守陳夤，夤擊破之。

translations.literal: Mountain Yue bandits in Danyang surrounded the governor Chen Yin; Yin struck and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Mountain Yue raiders in Danyang besieged Governor Chen Yin, who broke the siege and drove them off.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丁亥，中常侍侯覽諷有司奏前司空虞放、太僕杜密、長樂少府李膺、司隸校尉朱㝢、潁川太守巴肅、沛相荀昱、河內太守魏朗、山陽太守翟超皆為鉤黨，下獄，死者百餘人，妻子徙邊，諸附從者錮及五屬。

translations.literal: On dinghai of the tenth month in winter, Household Regular Attendant Hou Lan prompted the responsible officials to memorialize that former Minister of Works Yu Fang, Grand Coachman Du Mi, Junior Prefect of Chang'an Li Ying, Metropolitan Commandant Zhu Yu, Administrator of Yingchuan Ba Su, Chancellor of Pei Xun Yu, Administrator of Henei Wei Lang, Administrator of Shanyang Zhai Chao were all 'hooked faction' associates; they were imprisoned, and more than a hundred died; wives and children were moved to the frontier; those attached as followers were proscribed through five degrees of kinship.
translations.idiomatic: On dinghai in the tenth month the eunuch Hou Lan induced the court to denounce Yu Fang, Du Mi, Li Ying, Zhu Yu, Ba Su, Xun Yu, Wei Lang, and Zhai Chao as members of a proscribed faction; scores died in prison, families were banished to the frontier, and associates were barred from office through five degrees of relationship.

sentences.zh: 制詔州郡大舉鉤黨，於是天下豪桀及儒學行義者，一切結為黨人。

translations.literal: An edict ordered the provinces and commanderies to conduct a great round-up of faction associates, whereupon heroes of the empire and those who practiced Confucian learning and moral conduct were all bound as faction members.
translations.idiomatic: Provinces and commanderies were commanded to hunt down faction sympathizers on a vast scale, so that local notables and every scholar with a reputation for integrity could be labeled a partisan.

sentences.zh: 戊戌晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On wuxu, last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The month’s last day, wuxu, saw a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 十一月，太尉劉寵免，太僕郭禧為太尉。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Grand Commandant Liu Chong was dismissed, and Grand Coachman Guo Xi was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Liu Chong was removed as grand commandant; Guo Xi, grand coachman, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇并州。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided Bing province.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei horsemen raided Bing province.

sentences.zh: 是歲，長樂太僕曹節為車騎將軍，百餘日罷。

translations.literal: That year, Grand Coachman of Chang'an Cao Jiong was made General of Chariots and Cavalry; after more than a hundred days he was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: That year Cao Jiong, grand coachman of the Chang'an palace, briefly held the post of general of chariots and cavalry for just over a hundred days before being stood down.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月，河內人婦食夫，河南人夫食婦。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, first month, in Henei a wife ate her husband, in Henan a husband ate his wife.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year’s first month, famine drove a woman in Henei to devour her husband and a man in Henan to devour his wife.

sentences.zh: 三月丙寅晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On bingyin, last day of the third month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse occurred on the last day of the third month, bingyin.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，太尉郭禧罷，太中大夫聞人襲為太尉。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Grand Commandant Guo Xi was removed, and Grand Counselor Wenren Xi was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Guo Xi stepped down as grand commandant and Wenren Xi, grand counselor, took the post.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司空劉囂罷。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Minister of Works Liu Ao was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ao was dismissed as minister of works in the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 八月，大鴻臚橋玄為司空。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, Grand Herald Qiao Xuan was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Grand Herald Qiao Xuan became minister of works.

sentences.zh: 九月，執金吾董寵下獄死。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, Bearer of the Golden Mace Dong Chong was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Dong Chong, bearer of the golden mace, was jailed and died there.

sentences.zh: 冬，濟南賊起，攻東平陵。

translations.literal: In winter, bandits arose in Jinan and attacked Dongpingling.
translations.idiomatic: That winter rebels rose in Jinan and struck the county seat of Dongpingling.

sentences.zh: 鬱林烏滸民相率內屬。

translations.literal: The Wuhu people of Yulin came in a body to submit and be attached.
translations.idiomatic: The Wuhu people of Yulin commandery came over in a body to place themselves under Han rule.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月甲子，帝加元服，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, first month jiazi, the emperor added the capping-garment; he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi in the first month of the fourth year the emperor came of age with the capping ceremony, and a general amnesty followed.

sentences.zh: 賜公卿以下各有差，唯黨人不赦。

translations.literal: He bestowed on the ministers of state and below each according to degree, but faction members alone were not amnestied.
translations.idiomatic: Gifts were distributed down the official hierarchy by rank, but partisans of the proscribed faction were excluded from the amnesty.

sentences.zh: 二月癸卯，地震，海水溢，河水清。

translations.literal: On guimao of the second month, there was an earthquake; seawater overflowed; the Yellow River ran clear.
translations.idiomatic: On guimao in the second month the earth shook, the sea surged inland, and the Yellow River ran unseasonably clear.

sentences.zh: 三月辛酉朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On xinyou, new moon of the third month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The new moon on xinyou in the third month brought a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 太尉聞人襲免，太僕李咸為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Wenren Xi was dismissed, and Grand Coachman Li Xian was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Wenren Xi was dismissed as grand commandant; Li Xian, grand coachman, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 詔公卿至六百石各上封事。

translations.literal: He ordered the ministers of state down to six hundred shi each to submit sealed memorials.
translations.idiomatic: An edict required every official from the highest ministers down to the six-hundred-shi rank to file a sealed memorial.

sentences.zh: 大疫，使中謁者巡行致醫藥。

translations.literal: There was a great plague; he sent Palace Envoys on circuit to deliver medicine.
translations.idiomatic: A severe epidemic broke out, and palace envoys were dispatched with medicines to afflicted regions.

sentences.zh: 司徒許訓免，司空橋玄為司徒。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Xu Xun was dismissed, and Minister of Works Qiao Xuan was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Xun was removed as minister of education; Qiao Xuan left the ministry of works to succeed him.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，太常來豔為司空。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Minister of Ceremonies Lai Yan was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Minister of Ceremonies Lai Yan was appointed minister of works.

sentences.zh: 五月，河東地裂，雨雹，山水暴出。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, the earth split in Hedong, hail fell as rain, and mountain waters burst forth violently.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Hedong suffered ground fissures, hailstorms, and violent flash floods from the hills.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司空來豔免。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Minister of Works Lai Yan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Lai Yan was dismissed as minister of works in the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，立貴人宋氏為皇后。

translations.literal: On guichou, the Worthy Lady of the Song clan was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou the emperor elevated Worthy Lady Song to empress.

sentences.zh: 司徒橋玄免。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Qiao Xuan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Qiao Xuan was removed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 太常宗俱為司空，前司空許栩為司徒。

translations.literal: Minister of Ceremonies Zong Ju was made Minister of Works, former Minister of Works Xu Xu was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Zong Ju, minister of ceremonies, became minister of works, while former Minister of Works Xu Xu returned as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 冬，鮮卑寇并州。

translations.literal: In winter, the Xianbei raided Bing province.
translations.idiomatic: That winter Xianbei raiders struck Bing province again.

sentences.zh: 熹平元年春三月壬戌，太傅胡廣薨。

translations.literal: In the first year of Xiping, spring, third month renxu, Grand Tutor Hu Guang died.
translations.idiomatic: On renxu in the third month of the first Xiping year Grand Tutor Hu Guang died.

sentences.zh: 夏五月己巳，大赦天下，改元熹平。

translations.literal: In summer, fifth month yisi, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven and changed the reign title to Xiping.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi in the fifth month a general amnesty was proclaimed and the era name changed to Xiping.

sentences.zh: 長樂太僕侯覽有罪，自殺。

translations.literal: Grand Coachman of Chang'an Hou Lan, guilty of an offense, committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Hou Lan, grand coachman of the Chang'an palace, facing charges, took his own life.

sentences.zh: 六月，京師雨水。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, rain fell in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: Heavy rains again inundated the capital in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，皇太后竇氏崩。

translations.literal: On guisi, Empress Dowager née Dou died.
translations.idiomatic: On guisi Empress Dowager Dou died.

sentences.zh: 秋七月甲寅，葬桓思皇后。

translations.literal: In autumn, seventh month jiayin, Empress Dowager Huansi was buried.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the seventh month she was buried with the posthumous title Empress Dowager Huansi.

sentences.zh: 宦官諷司隸校尉段颎捕繫太學諸生千餘人。

translations.literal: Eunuchs prompted Metropolitan Commandant Duan Jiong to arrest and bind more than a thousand students of the Imperial Academy.
translations.idiomatic: The eunuchs induced Metropolitan Commandant Duan Jiong to round up over a thousand Imperial University students.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，渤海王悝被誣謀反，丁亥，悝及妻子皆自殺。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Prince Hui of Bohai, Kui, was falsely accused of plotting rebellion; on dinghai, Kui and his wife and children all committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Liu Kui, Prince Hui of Bohai, was framed for treason; on dinghai he and his family killed themselves.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，大疫，使使者巡行致醫藥。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, first month, there was a great plague; he sent envoys on circuit to deliver medicine.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year’s first month another great plague raged, and imperial envoys were sent with medicines on relief circuits.

sentences.zh: 丁丑，司空宗俱薨。

translations.literal: On dingchou, Minister of Works Zong Ju died.
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Works Zong Ju died on dingchou.

sentences.zh: 二月壬午，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On renwu of the second month, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu in the second month a general amnesty was granted.

sentences.zh: 以光祿勳楊賜為司空。

translations.literal: Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Yang Ci was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ci, superintendent of the household for imperial proceedings, was named minister of works.

sentences.zh: 三月，太尉李咸免。

translations.literal: In the third month, Grand Commandant Li Xian was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Li Xian was dismissed as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 夏五月，以司隸校尉段颎為太尉。

translations.literal: In summer, the fifth month, Metropolitan Commandant Duan Jiong was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Metropolitan Commandant Duan Jiong was promoted to grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 沛相師遷坐誣罔國王，下獄死。

translations.literal: Chancellor of Pei Shi Qian sat guilty of deceiving the king of the kingdom unjustly; he was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Shi Qian, chancellor of Pei, was convicted of slandering his prince and died in prison.

sentences.zh: 六月，北海地震。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, there was an earthquake in Beihai.
translations.idiomatic: Beihai commandery was shaken by an earthquake in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 東萊、北海海水溢。

translations.literal: In Donglai and Beihai, seawater overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: The sea breached its banks and flooded Donglai and Beihai.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司空楊賜免，太常潁川唐珍為司空。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Minister of Works Yang Ci was dismissed, and Minister of Ceremonies Tang Zhen of Yingchuan was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Yang Ci left the ministry of works; Tang Zhen of Yingchuan, minister of ceremonies, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 冬十二月，日南徼外國重譯貢獻。

translations.literal: In winter, the twelfth month, states beyond the frontier of Rinan came through layered interpretation to offer tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month embassies from countries beyond Rinan’s southern frontier arrived, relayed through interpreters, bearing tribute.

sentences.zh: 太尉段颎罷。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Duan Jiong was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Duan Jiong was dismissed as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇幽、幷二州。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided the two provinces of You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei forces raided both You and Bing provinces.

sentences.zh: 癸酉晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On guiyou, last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The month closed on guiyou with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月，夫餘國遣使貢獻。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, first month, the state of Puyu sent envoys with tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year’s first month the kingdom of Puyu sent envoys with gifts.

sentences.zh: 二月己巳，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On yisi of the second month, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi in the second month another general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 冬十月癸丑，令天下繫囚罪未決，入縑贖。

translations.literal: On guichou of the tenth month in winter, he ordered that prisoners in the empire whose crimes were not yet decided might enter silk brocade to redeem themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou in the tenth month undecided prisoners empire-wide were permitted to buy release with silk payments.

sentences.zh: 四年春三月，詔諸儒正《五經》文字，刻石立于太學門外。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, third month, he ordered the scholars to correct the text of the Five Classics and had them carved in stone standing outside the gate of the Imperial Academy.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month of the fourth year scholars were ordered to fix the canonical text of the Five Classics; the approved version was engraved on stone tablets set outside the university gate.

sentences.zh: 封河間王建子佗為任城王。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed Tuo, son of the prince of Hejian, Jian, as king of Rencheng.
translations.idiomatic: Tuo, son of Prince Jian of Hejian, was enfeoffed as king of Rencheng.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，郡國七大水。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, seven commanderies and kingdoms had great floods.
translations.idiomatic: Seven commanderies and kingdoms suffered serious flooding in the fourth month.

sentences.zh: 五月丁卯，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On dingmao of the fifth month, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On dingmao in the fifth month a general amnesty was issued.

sentences.zh: 延陵園灾，遣使者持節告祠延陵。

translations.literal: Yanling Park caught fire; he sent an envoy bearing staff of office to announce the affair and sacrifice at Yanling.
translations.idiomatic: Fire broke out at the Yanling park complex; an envoy with imperial staff was sent to report the disaster and offer sacrifice at Yanling.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇幽州。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided You province.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei raiders struck You province.

sentences.zh: 六月，弘農、三輔螟。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, there were locusts in Hongnong and the Three Adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts devastated Hongnong and the metropolitan region in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 遣守宮令之鹽監，穿渠為民興利。

translations.literal: He sent the Commandant of the Palace to the salt monopolies to dig canals for the people's benefit.
translations.idiomatic: The commandant of the palace was sent to government salt works to dig irrigation channels for the public good.

sentences.zh: 令郡國遇災者，減田租之半；

translations.literal: He ordered that when commanderies and kingdoms encountered disaster, they were to reduce the field land tax by half;
translations.idiomatic: Commanderies and kingdoms hit by natural disaster were ordered to cut the land tax by half.

sentences.zh: 其傷害十四以上，勿收責。

translations.literal: As for those whose harm reached fourteen or above, do not collect liability.
translations.idiomatic: Where disaster had destroyed forty percent or more of the crop, no land tax was to be collected on the lost portion.

sentences.zh: 冬十月丁巳，令天下繫囚罪未決，入縑贖。

translations.literal: On dingsi of the tenth month in winter, he ordered that prisoners in the empire whose crimes were not yet decided might enter silk brocade to redeem themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi in the tenth month undecided prisoners throughout the realm were again allowed to commute with silk payments.

sentences.zh: 拜沖帝母虞美人為憲園貴人，質帝母陳夫人為渤海孝王妃。

translations.literal: He invested the mother of Emperor Chong, the Honored Lady Yu, as Honored Lady of the Xian Garden, and the mother of Emperor Zhi, Lady Chen, as consort of Prince Xiao of Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: The mother of Emperor Chong, Honored Lady Yu, was titled Honored Lady of the Xian Garden estate; the mother of Emperor Zhi, Lady Chen, was given the title consort to Prince Xiao of Bohai.

sentences.zh: 改平準為中準，使宦者為令，列於內署。

translations.literal: He changed the Office of Equitable Marketing to Central Marketing, had eunuchs serve as directors, and arrayed them in the inner offices.
translations.idiomatic: The Office of Equitable Marketing was renamed Central Marketing; eunuchs were appointed as its directors and stationed in the inner palace bureaus.

sentences.zh: 自是諸署悉以閹人為丞、令。

translations.literal: From this time all offices uniformly used castrated men as assistants and directors.
translations.idiomatic: Henceforth every such agency had eunuchs filling the posts of assistant and director.

sentences.zh: 五年夏四月癸亥，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, summer, fourth month guihai, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On guihai in the fourth month of the fifth year a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 益州郡夷叛，太守李颙討平之。

translations.literal: The Yi of Yizhou commandery rebelled; the governor Li Yong campaigned and pacified them.
translations.idiomatic: The tribesmen of Yizhou commandery rose in revolt; Governor Li Yong suppressed the uprising.

sentences.zh: 復崇高山名為嵩高山。

translations.literal: He again honored the name of Lofty Mountain as Mount Song the Lofty.
translations.idiomatic: Mount Chong was again officially styled Mount Song, the Lofty Central Peak.

sentences.zh: 大雩。

translations.literal: He performed the great rain prayer.
translations.idiomatic: The court held the great rain sacrifice.

sentences.zh: 使侍御史行詔獄亭部，理冤枉，原輕繫，休囚徒。

translations.literal: He sent attendant censors to go through the imperial-prison precincts and village divisions, set right wrongful cases, release those lightly bound, and rest convicts.
translations.idiomatic: Attendant censors were sent to review imperial jails and local lockups, redress miscarriages of justice, free minor offenders, and give labor gangs respite.

sentences.zh: 五月，太尉陳耽罷，司空許訓為太尉。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, Grand Commandant Chen Dan was removed, and Minister of Works Xu Xun was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Chen Dan left the grand commandant’s post; Xu Xun, minister of works, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 閏月，永昌太守曹鸞坐訟黨人，弃市。

translations.literal: In the intercalary month, the governor of Yongchang, Cao Luan, sat guilty of litigating on behalf of faction members; he was executed in the market.
translations.idiomatic: During the intercalary month Cao Luan, governor of Yongchang, was put to death in the marketplace for having memorialized on behalf of the proscribed faction.

sentences.zh: 詔黨人門生、故吏、父兄、子弟在位者，皆免官禁錮。

translations.literal: He ordered that faction members’ disciples, former subordinates, fathers, elder brothers, sons, and younger brothers who held office were all dismissed from office and proscribed.
translations.idiomatic: An edict stripped from office and barred from service every serving official who was a student, former client, father, brother, son, or nephew of a listed partisan.

sentences.zh: 六月壬戌，太常南陽劉逸為司空。

translations.literal: On renxu of the sixth month, Minister of Ceremonies Liu Yi of Nanyang was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On renxu in the sixth month Liu Yi of Nanyang, minister of ceremonies, was appointed minister of works.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，太尉許訓罷，光祿勳劉寬為太尉。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Grand Commandant Xu Xun was removed, and Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Liu Kuan was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Xu Xun was dismissed as grand commandant; Liu Kuan, superintendent of the household for imperial proceedings, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 冬十月壬午，御殿後槐樹自拔倒豎。

translations.literal: On renwu of the tenth month in winter, behind the imperial hall the locust tree uprooted itself and stood inverted.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu in the tenth month a locust tree behind the throne hall tore itself from the ground and stood roots upward.

sentences.zh: 司徒袁隗罷。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Yuan Kui was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Kui was dismissed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 十一月丙戌，光祿大夫楊賜為司徒。

translations.literal: On bingxu of the eleventh month, Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Yang Ci was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On bingxu in the eleventh month Yang Ci, grandee of brilliant candidacy, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 十二月，甘陵王定薨。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, Prince Ding of Ganling died.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Ding of Ganling died in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 試太學生年六十以上百餘人，除郎中、太子舍人至王家郎、郡國文學吏。

translations.literal: He examined more than a hundred Imperial Academy students aged sixty and above, and appointed them gentlemen consultants, householders for the heir apparent, down to gentlemen of the princely houses and literary clerks of commanderies and kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: Over a hundred Imperial University students past sixty were examined and given appointments ranging from gentleman consultant and heir’s household attendant to princely household gentleman and commandery literary officer.

sentences.zh: 是歲，鮮卑寇幽州。

translations.literal: That year, the Xianbei raided You province.
translations.idiomatic: That year Xianbei raiders struck You province.

sentences.zh: 沛國言黃龍見譙。

translations.literal: Pei kingdom reported a yellow dragon seen at Qiao.
translations.idiomatic: Pei kingdom reported the sighting of a yellow dragon at Qiao.

sentences.zh: 六年春正月辛丑，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, spring, first month xinchou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou in the first month of the sixth year a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 二月，南宮平城門及武庫東垣屋自壞。

translations.literal: In the second month, the Pingcheng Gate of the Southern Palace and the eastern wall buildings of the armory collapsed of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month the Pingcheng Gate of the Southern Palace and the eastern wall of the armory fell in without human agency.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，大旱，七州蝗。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, there was great drought; seven provinces had locusts.
translations.idiomatic: The fourth month brought severe drought and locust swarms across seven provinces.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇三邊。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided the three frontiers.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei horsemen raided the northern frontier on a broad front.

sentences.zh: 市賈民為宣陵孝子者數十人，皆除太子舍人。

translations.literal: Several tens of market-trader commoners who acted as filial sons for Xuan Mausoleum were all appointed householders for the heir apparent.
translations.idiomatic: Dozens of merchants who had posed as mourners-for-hire at Emperor Huan’s tomb were enrolled as the heir’s household attendants.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，司空劉逸免，衛尉陳球為司空。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Minister of Works Liu Yi was dismissed, and Commandant of the Guards Chen Qiu was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Liu Yi was removed as minister of works; Chen Qiu, commandant of the guards, took the post.

sentences.zh: 八月，遣破鮮卑中郎將田晏出雲中，使匈奴中郎將臧旻與南單于出鴈門，護烏桓校尉夏育出高柳，並伐鮮卑，晏等大敗。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, he sent General of the Household for All Purposes Who Smashes the Xianbei Tian Yan out from Yunzhong, ordered Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Zang Min together with the Southern Shanyu out from Yanmen, and Colonel Protecting the Wuhuan Xia Yu out from Gaoliu, jointly to campaign against the Xianbei; Tian Yan and the others were greatly defeated.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month three columns marched against the Xianbei: Tian Yan from Yunzhong, Zang Min with the Southern Shanyu from Yanmen, and Xia Yu, colonel protecting the Wuhuan, from Gaoliu; all three forces suffered crushing defeat.

sentences.zh: 冬十月癸丑朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On guichou, new moon of the tenth month in winter, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse occurred on the new moon, guichou, in the tenth month.

sentences.zh: 太尉劉寬免。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Liu Kuan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Kuan was dismissed as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 帝臨辟雍。

translations.literal: The emperor attended the Hall of the Circular Moat.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor presided at the imperial academy’s circular moat hall.

sentences.zh: 辛丑，京師地震。

translations.literal: On xinchou, there was an earthquake in the capital.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou the capital region was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 辛亥，令天下繫囚罪未決，入縑贖。

translations.literal: On xinhai, he ordered that prisoners in the empire whose crimes were not yet decided might enter silk brocade to redeem themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai undecided prisoners were again permitted silk commutations.

sentences.zh: 十一月，司空陳球免。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Minister of Works Chen Qiu was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Qiu was removed as minister of works in the eleventh month.

sentences.zh: 十二月甲寅，太常河南孟□為太尉。

translations.literal: On jiayin of the twelfth month, Minister of Ceremonies Meng □ of Henan was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On jiayin in the twelfth month Meng Yu of Henan, minister of ceremonies, became grand commandant; the received text omits one character of his name.

sentences.zh: 庚辰，司徒楊賜免。

translations.literal: On gengchen, Minister of Education Yang Ci was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen Yang Ci was dismissed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 太常陳耽為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Ceremonies Chen Dan was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Dan, minister of ceremonies, was appointed minister of works.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇遼西。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided Liaoxi.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei raiders invaded Liaoxi.

sentences.zh: 永安太僕王旻下獄死。

translations.literal: Grand Coachman of Yong'an Wang Min was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Min, grand coachman of the Yong'an palace, was jailed and died there.

sentences.zh: 光和五年春正月，合浦、交阯烏滸蠻叛，招引九真、日南民攻沒郡縣。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Guanghe, spring, first month, the Wuhu Man of Hepu and Jiaozhi rebelled, summoned and led the people of Jiuzhen and Rinan, and attacked and drowned commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the fifth Guanghe year the Wuhu tribes of Hepu and Jiaozhi rose in revolt, drew in people from Jiuzhen and Rinan, and overran county seats.

sentences.zh: 太尉孟□罷。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Meng □ was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Commandant Meng Yu was dismissed; the received text omits one character of his name.

sentences.zh: 二月辛亥朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On xinhai, new moon of the second month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The second month opened on xinhai with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 癸丑，光祿勳陳國袁滂為司徒。

translations.literal: On guichou, Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Yuan Pang of Chen was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: On guichou Yuan Pang of Chen, superintendent of the household for imperial proceedings, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 己未，地震。

translations.literal: On jiwei, there was an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On jiwei the earth shook.

sentences.zh: 始置鴻都門學生。

translations.literal: For the first time he established students of the Hongdu Gate Academy.
translations.idiomatic: Students were first enrolled at the Hongdu Gate literary academy.

sentences.zh: 三月辛丑，大赦天下，改元光和。

translations.literal: On xinchou of the third month, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven and changed the reign title to Guanghe.
translations.idiomatic: On xinchou in the third month a general amnesty was proclaimed and the era name changed to Guanghe.

sentences.zh: 太常常山張顥為太尉。

translations.literal: Minister of Ceremonies Zhang Hao of Changshan was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Hao of Changshan, minister of ceremonies, was appointed grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙辰，地震。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month bingchen, there was an earthquake.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen in the fourth month another earthquake struck.

sentences.zh: 侍中寺雌雞化為雄。

translations.literal: In the Palace Attendants office a hen changed into a cock.
translations.idiomatic: At the palace attendants’ compound a hen turned into a rooster.

sentences.zh: 司空陳耽免，太常來豔為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Chen Dan was dismissed, and Minister of Ceremonies Lai Yan was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Dan was removed as minister of works; Lai Yan, minister of ceremonies, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 五月壬午，有白衣人入德陽殿門，亡去不獲。

translations.literal: On renwu of the fifth month, there was a person in white who entered the Deyang Hall gate and fled; he was not caught.
translations.idiomatic: On renwu a figure in white slipped through the Deyang Hall gate and vanished; searchers never found him.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，屯騎校尉袁逢為司空。

translations.literal: In the tenth month in winter, Colonel of the Garrison Cavalry Yuan Feng was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Yuan Feng, colonel of the garrison cavalry, became minister of works.

sentences.zh: 皇后宋氏廢，后父執金吾酆下獄死。

translations.literal: Empress née Song was deposed; the empress’s father, Bearer of the Golden Mace Feng, was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Empress Song was deposed; her father Feng, bearer of the golden mace, was thrown into prison and died there.

sentences.zh: 丙子晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On bingzi, last day of the month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The month ended on bingzi with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 十一月，太尉陳球免。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Grand Commandant Chen Qiu was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Qiu was dismissed as grand commandant in the eleventh month.

sentences.zh: 十二月丁巳，光祿大夫橋玄為太尉。

translations.literal: On dingsi of the twelfth month, Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Qiao Xuan was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On dingsi in the twelfth month Qiao Xuan, grandee of brilliant candidacy, became grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 是歲，鮮卑寇酒泉。

translations.literal: That year, the Xianbei raided Jiuquan.
translations.idiomatic: That year Xianbei raiders struck Jiuquan commandery.

sentences.zh: 京師馬生人。

translations.literal: In the capital a horse gave birth to a human.
translations.idiomatic: In the capital a mare was reported to have foaled something human-shaped.

sentences.zh: 初開西邸賣官，自關內侯、虎賁、羽林，入錢各有差。

translations.literal: For the first time he opened the Western Lodge to sell offices, from marquis within the passes, tiger lancers, and Feathered Forest, the entry of cash each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: Office-selling began at the Western Lodge: ranks from marquis-within-the-passes down through tiger lancer and Feathered Forest posts carried fixed cash prices.

sentences.zh: 私令左右賣公卿，公千萬，卿五百萬。

translations.literal: Privately he ordered his intimates to sell minister and excellency posts: excellency ten million, minister five million.
translations.idiomatic: Privately he had his favorites peddle ministerial posts—ten million cash for a seat among the three excellencies, five million for the nine ministers.

sentences.zh: 二年春，大疫，使常侍、中謁者巡行致醫藥。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, there was a great plague; he sent regular attendants and palace envoys on circuit to deliver medicine.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year’s spring a severe plague broke out; regular attendants and palace envoys carried medicines on relief tours.

sentences.zh: 三月，司徒袁滂免，大鴻臚劉郃為司徒。

translations.literal: In the third month, Minister of Education Yuan Pang was dismissed, and Grand Herald Liu He was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Yuan Pang left the ministry of education; Grand Herald Liu He succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 乙丑，太尉橋玄罷，太中大夫段颎為太尉。

translations.literal: On yichou, Grand Commandant Qiao Xuan was removed, and Grand Counselor Duan Jiong was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: On yichou Qiao Xuan stepped down as grand commandant; Duan Jiong, grand counselor, took his place.

sentences.zh: 京兆地震。

translations.literal: There was an earthquake in the capital district.
translations.idiomatic: The metropolitan district was shaken by an earthquake.

sentences.zh: 司空袁逢罷，太常張濟為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Yuan Feng was removed, and Minister of Ceremonies Zhang Ji was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Feng was dismissed as minister of works; Zhang Ji, minister of ceremonies, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 夏四月甲戌朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On jiaxu, new moon of the fourth month in summer, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The new moon on jiaxu in the fourth month was eclipsed.

sentences.zh: 辛巳，中常侍王甫及太尉段颎並下獄死。

translations.literal: On xinsi, Household Regular Attendant Wang Fu and Grand Commandant Duan Jiong were both imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi the eunuch Wang Fu and Grand Commandant Duan Jiong were jailed and died there.

sentences.zh: 丁酉，大赦天下，諸黨人禁錮小功以下皆除之。

translations.literal: On dingyou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven; for all faction members proscribed, those within lesser mourning and below were all removed from the prohibition.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou a general amnesty lifted the partisan ban for everyone within the lesser-gong degree of kinship or more distant.

sentences.zh: 東平王端薨。

translations.literal: Prince Duan of Dongping died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Duan, prince of Dongping, died.

sentences.zh: 五月，衛尉劉寬為太尉。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, Commandant of the Guards Liu Kuan was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Liu Kuan, commandant of the guards, was promoted to grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，使匈奴中郎將張脩有罪，下獄死。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Colonel in Charge of the Xiongnu Zhang Xiu, guilty of an offense, was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Zhang Xiu, colonel in charge of the Xiongnu, was jailed on charges and died in prison.

sentences.zh: 冬十月甲申，司徒劉郃、永樂少府陳球、衛尉陽球、步兵校尉劉納謀誅宦者，事泄，皆下獄死。

translations.literal: On jiashen of the tenth month in winter, Minister of Education Liu He, Junior Prefect of Yongle Chen Qiu, Commandant of the Guards Yang Qiu, and Colonel of the Foot Soldiers Liu Na plotted to execute the eunuchs; the affair leaked; all were imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: On jiashen in the tenth month Liu He, Chen Qiu, Yang Qiu, and Liu Na conspired to kill the eunuchs; the plot was exposed and all four died in prison.

sentences.zh: 巴郡板楯蠻叛，遣御史中丞蕭瑗督益州刺史討之，不克。

translations.literal: The Ban-dun Man of Ba commandery rebelled; he sent Palace Assistant Secretary Xiao Yuan to supervise the Inspector of Yi province in campaigning against them; he did not overcome them.
translations.idiomatic: The Ban-dun tribesmen of Ba commandery rebelled; Palace Assistant Secretary Xiao Yuan oversaw the inspector of Yi province in a campaign that failed to crush them.

sentences.zh: 十二月，光祿勳楊賜為司徒。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Yang Ci was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Yang Ci, superintendent of the household for imperial proceedings, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇幽、幷二州。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided the two provinces of You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei raiders struck You and Bing provinces.

sentences.zh: 是歲，河間王利薨。

translations.literal: That year, Prince Li of Hejian died.
translations.idiomatic: That year Liu Li, prince of Hejian, died.

sentences.zh: 洛陽女子生兒，兩頭四臂。

translations.literal: A woman of Luoyang gave birth to a child with two heads and four arms.
translations.idiomatic: A woman in Luoyang bore a child reported to have two heads and four arms.

sentences.zh: 三年春正月癸酉，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, first month guiyou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou in the first month of the third year a general amnesty was granted.

sentences.zh: 二月，公府駐駕廡自壞。

translations.literal: In the second month, the sheds for halting the imperial carriage at the excellencies’ offices collapsed of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month the carriage sheds at the Three Excellencies compound collapsed on their own.

sentences.zh: 三月，梁王元薨。

translations.literal: In the third month, Prince Yuan of Liang died.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Yuan of Liang died in the third month.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，江夏蠻叛。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, the Jiangxia Man rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: The Man tribes of Jiangxia rose in revolt in the fourth month.

sentences.zh: 六月，詔公卿舉能通《古文尚書》、《毛詩》、《左氏》、《穀梁春秋》各一人，悉除議郎。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, he ordered the ministers of state to recommend one man each able to master the Old Text "Shang shu", the "Mao Odes", the "Zuo Tradition", and the "Guliang Spring and Autumn"; all were appointed gentlemen consultants.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month the court called for one nominee apiece expert in the Old Text "Shang shu", the "Mao Odes", the "Zuo Tradition", and the "Guliang Spring and Autumn"; each man chosen was appointed gentleman consultant.

sentences.zh: 秋，表是地震，涌水出。

translations.literal: In autumn, there was an earthquake at Biaoshi, and gushing water came forth.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn Biaoshi county was hit by an earthquake that brought groundwater surging to the surface.

sentences.zh: 八月，令繫囚罪未決，入縑贖，各有差。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, he ordered that bound prisoners whose crimes were not yet decided might enter silk brocade to redeem themselves, each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month undecided prisoners could again buy release with graded silk payments.

sentences.zh: 冬閏月，有星孛於狼、弧。

translations.literal: In winter, intercalary month, there was a broom star in Lang and Hu.
translations.idiomatic: During the intercalary winter month a comet appeared in the Bow and Arrow constellations.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇幽、幷二州。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided the two provinces of You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei raiders again struck You and Bing.

sentences.zh: 十二月己巳，立貴人何氏為皇后。

translations.literal: On jisi of the twelfth month, the Worthy Lady née He was established as empress.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi in the twelfth month Worthy Lady He was elevated to empress.

sentences.zh: 是歲，作罼圭、靈昆苑。

translations.literal: That year, he built Bigui and Lingkun parks.
translations.idiomatic: That year work began on the Bigui and Lingkun palace parks.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月，初置騄驥廄丞，領受郡國調馬。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, first month, for the first time he established an assistant for the Swift Steeds stable to receive and take charge of horses levied from commanderies and kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the fourth year the post of assistant to the Swift Steeds stable was created to handle horse levies from the commanderies and kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 豪右辜搉，馬一匹至二百萬。

translations.literal: Powerful families monopolized and cornered the market; one horse reached two million.
translations.idiomatic: Rich magnates cornered the trade until a single horse could cost two million cash.

sentences.zh: 二月，郡國上芝英草。

translations.literal: In the second month, commanderies and kingdoms presented zhi-ying grass.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month local authorities reported finding auspicious “spirit-fungus” plants.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚子，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In summer, fourth month gengzi, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi in the fourth month a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 交阯刺史朱儁討交阯、合浦烏滸蠻，破之。

translations.literal: Inspector of Jiaozhi Zhu Jun campaigned against the Wuhu Man of Jiaozhi and Hepu and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Jun, inspector of Jiaozhi, campaigned against the Wuhu tribes of Jiaozhi and Hepu and broke their resistance.

sentences.zh: 六月庚辰，雨雹。

translations.literal: On gengchen of the sixth month, hail fell as rain.
translations.idiomatic: On gengchen in the sixth month a hailstorm struck.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，河南言鳳皇見新城，群鳥隨之；

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Henan reported a phoenix seen at Xincheng, and flocking birds followed it;
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Henan commandery reported a phoenix at Xincheng with a train of other birds;

sentences.zh: 賜新城令及三老、力田帛，各有差。

translations.literal: he bestowed silks on the magistrate of Xincheng and the elders and the exert-fielders, each according to degree.
translations.idiomatic: Rewards in silk were given in graded amounts to the magistrate of Xincheng, the local elders, and model farmers.

sentences.zh: 九月庚寅朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On gengyin, new moon of the ninth month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The ninth month opened on gengyin with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 太尉劉寬免，衛尉許□為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Liu Kuan was dismissed, and Commandant of the Guards Xu □ was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Kuan was removed as grand commandant; Xu Xiang, commandant of the guards, succeeded him; the received text omits one character of his name.

sentences.zh: 閏月辛酉，北宮東掖庭永巷署災。

translations.literal: On xinyou of the intercalary month, the Eternal Lane office of the eastern side court of the Northern Palace caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou in the intercalary month fire destroyed the Eternal Lane office in the Northern Palace’s eastern annex.

sentences.zh: 司徒楊賜罷。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Yang Ci was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Yang Ci was dismissed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，太常陳耽為司徒。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Minister of Ceremonies Chen Dan was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Chen Dan, minister of ceremonies, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇幽、幷二州。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided the two provinces of You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei raiders struck You and Bing provinces again.

sentences.zh: 是歲，帝作列肆於後宮，使諸采女販賣，更相盜竊爭鬥。

translations.literal: That year, the emperor set up market rows in the rear palace, had the various selected women buy and sell, and they stole from one another and fought.
translations.idiomatic: That year the emperor opened bazaar lanes in the harem and set the palace ladies to hawking wares; they pilfered from one another and came to blows.

sentences.zh: 帝著商估服，飲宴為樂。

translations.literal: The emperor put on merchant peddler garments and took drinking feasts as his pleasure.
translations.idiomatic: He dressed like a street peddler and amused himself with feasting and wine.

sentences.zh: 又於西園弄狗，著進賢冠，帶綬。

translations.literal: Again in the Western Garden he played with dogs, put on the cap of advanced worthies, and wore official ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: At the Western Garden he sported with dogs, crowned himself with the cap of advanced worthies, and fastened ministerial ribbons about his waist.

sentences.zh: 又駕四驢，帝躬自操轡，驅馳周旋，京師轉相放效。

translations.literal: Again he drove a team of four donkeys; the emperor personally took the reins, galloped in circles, and the capital in turn imitated him.
translations.idiomatic: He also raced a four-donkey chariot, handling the reins himself while careering about the grounds; the capital’s nobles rushed to copy the fad.

sentences.zh: 五年春正月辛未，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, spring, first month xinwei, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei in the first month of the fifth year a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 二月，大疫。

translations.literal: In the second month, there was a great plague.
translations.idiomatic: A severe epidemic raged in the second month.

sentences.zh: 三月，司徒陳耽免。

translations.literal: In the third month, Minister of Education Chen Dan was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Dan was removed as minister of education in the third month.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，旱。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, there was drought.
translations.idiomatic: The fourth month brought drought.

sentences.zh: 太常袁隗為司徒。

translations.literal: Minister of Ceremonies Yuan Kui was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Kui, minister of ceremonies, succeeded as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 五月庚申，永樂宮署灾。

translations.literal: On gengshen of the fifth month, the Yongle Palace offices caught fire.
translations.idiomatic: Fire destroyed offices at the Yongle Palace on gengshen in the fifth month.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，有星孛於太微。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, there was a broom star in the Supreme Enclosure.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in the Supreme Enclosure constellation in the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 巴郡板楯蠻詣太守曹謙降。

translations.literal: The Ban-dun Man of Ba commandery came to Governor Cao Qian and surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: The Ban-dun tribesmen of Ba commandery submitted to Governor Cao Qian.

sentences.zh: 癸酉，令繫囚罪未決，入縑贖。

translations.literal: On guiyou, he ordered that bound prisoners whose crimes were not yet decided might enter silk brocade to redeem themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On guiyou undecided prisoners were permitted silk commutations.

sentences.zh: 八月，起四百尺觀於阿亭道。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, he raised a four-hundred-chi observation tower on the Ating road.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month a four-hundred-foot lookout tower was erected on the Ating road.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，太尉許□罷，太常楊賜為太尉。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Grand Commandant Xu □ was removed, and Minister of Ceremonies Yang Ci was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Xu Xiang stepped down as grand commandant; Yang Ci, minister of ceremonies, replaced him; the received text omits one character of Xu’s name.

sentences.zh: 校獵上林苑，歷函谷關，遂巡狩於廣成苑。

translations.literal: He conducted a battue hunt in Shanglin Park, passed through Hangu Pass, and then carried out a hunt tour at Guangcheng Park.
translations.idiomatic: He hunted in Shanglin Park, rode through Hangu Pass, and continued with a ritual hunt at Guangcheng Park.

sentences.zh: 十二月，還，幸太學。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, he returned and visited the Imperial Academy.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month he returned to the capital and visited the Imperial University.

sentences.zh: 六年春正月，日南徼外國重譯貢獻。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, spring, first month, states beyond the frontier of Rinan came through layered interpretation to offer tribute.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the sixth year embassies from beyond Rinan arrived with tribute.

sentences.zh: 二月，復長陵縣，比豐、沛。

translations.literal: In the second month, he restored Changling county, treating it like Feng and Pei.
translations.idiomatic: Changling county was re-established in the second month with privileges matching those of the Gaozu’s home counties.

sentences.zh: 三月辛未，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On xinwei of the third month, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei in the third month a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 夏，大旱。

translations.literal: In summer, there was great drought.
translations.idiomatic: That summer brought severe drought.

sentences.zh: 秋，金城河水溢。

translations.literal: In autumn, the waters of the Yellow River in Jincheng overflowed.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn the Yellow River burst its banks in Jincheng.

sentences.zh: 五原山岸崩。

translations.literal: The mountain banks in Wuyuan collapsed.
translations.idiomatic: Cliffs along the Wuyuan frontier gave way.

sentences.zh: 始置圃囿署，以宦者為令。

translations.literal: For the first time he established a Park and Preserve Office, with a eunuch as director.
translations.idiomatic: A new Park and Preserve bureau was created under a eunuch director.

sentences.zh: 冬，東海、東萊、珢邪井中冰厚尺餘。

translations.literal: In winter, in Donghai, Donglai, and Langya, ice in wells was more than a chi thick.
translations.idiomatic: That winter wells in Donghai, Donglai, and Langya froze to a depth of over a foot.

sentences.zh: 大有年。

translations.literal: There was a great harvest year.
translations.idiomatic: The year brought bumper harvests.

sentences.zh: 中平元年春二月，鉅鹿人張角自稱『黃天』，其部帥有三十六方，皆著黃巾，同日反叛。

translations.literal: In the first year of Zhongping, spring, second month, Zhang Jue of Julu styled himself Yellow Heaven; his division commanders had thirty-six hosts, all wore yellow turbans, and on the same day they rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the first Zhongping year Zhang Jue of Julu proclaimed himself agent of Yellow Heaven; his army was organized in thirty-six divisions whose men all tied on yellow turbans and rose together.

sentences.zh: 安平、甘陵人各執其王以應之。

translations.literal: The people of Anping and Ganling each seized their kings to respond to them.
translations.idiomatic: In Anping and Ganling the commoners seized their princes and joined the rebellion.

sentences.zh: 三月戊申，以河南尹何進為大將軍，將兵屯都亭。

translations.literal: On wushen of the third month, Henan Governor He Jin was made General-in-Chief and led troops to encamp at the capital pavilions.
translations.idiomatic: On wushen in the third month He Jin, governor of Henan, was appointed general-in-chief and encamped his troops at the metropolitan pavilions.

sentences.zh: 置八關都尉官。

translations.literal: He established offices of commandants for the eight passes.
translations.idiomatic: Eight new pass commandantships were created.

sentences.zh: 壬子，大赦天下黨人，還諸徙者，唯張角不赦。

translations.literal: On renzi, he issued a great amnesty for faction members under Heaven, returned the various exiles, but Zhang Jue alone was not amnestied.
translations.idiomatic: On renzi partisans empire-wide were pardoned and exiles recalled, while Zhang Jue alone remained outside the amnesty.

sentences.zh: 詔公卿出馬、弩，舉列將子孫及吏民有明戰陣之略者，詣公車。

translations.literal: He ordered the ministers of state to furnish horses and crossbows, and to recommend descendants of arrayed generals and clerks and commoners who had clear mastery of battle formations, to present themselves at the Palace Coaches Office.
translations.idiomatic: The court demanded horses and crossbows from the high ministers and called for volunteers with military talent, including descendants of noted generals, to report to the Palace Coaches office.

sentences.zh: 遣北中郎將盧植討張角，左中郎將皇甫嵩、右中郎將朱儁討潁川黃巾。

translations.literal: He sent General of the Household for the North Lu Zhi to campaign against Zhang Jue, and General of the Household for the Left Huangfu Song and General of the Household for the Right Zhu Jun to campaign against the Yellow Turbans of Yingchuan.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Zhi, general of the household for the north, was sent against Zhang Jue, while Huangfu Song and Zhu Jun, generals of the household for the left and right, marched on the Yingchuan Yellow Turbans.

sentences.zh: 庚子，南陽黃巾張曼成攻殺郡守褚貢。

translations.literal: On gengzi, the Nanyang Yellow Turban Zhang Mancheng attacked and killed the governor Chu Gong.
translations.idiomatic: On gengzi Zhang Mancheng’s Yellow Turbans in Nanyang slew the governor, Chu Gong.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，太尉楊賜免，太僕弘農鄧盛為太尉。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Grand Commandant Yang Ci was dismissed, and Grand Coachman Deng Sheng of Hongnong was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Yang Ci left the grand commandant’s post; Deng Sheng of Hongnong, grand coachman, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 司空張濟罷，大司農張溫為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Zhang Ji was removed, and Grand Minister of Agriculture Zhang Wen was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ji was dismissed as minister of works; Zhang Wen, grand minister of agriculture, took his place.

sentences.zh: 朱儁為黃巾波才所敗。

translations.literal: Zhu Jun was defeated by the Yellow Turban Bo Cai.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu Jun suffered defeat at the hands of the Yellow Turban leader Bo Cai.

sentences.zh: 侍中向栩、張鈞坐言宦者，下獄死。

translations.literal: Palace Attendants Xiang Xu and Zhang Jun sat guilty of speaking about the eunuchs; they were imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Xu and Zhang Jun, palace attendants, were jailed and died for having criticized the eunuchs.

sentences.zh: 汝南黃巾敗太守趙謙於邵陵。

translations.literal: The Runan Yellow Turbans defeated Governor Zhao Qian at Shaoling.
translations.idiomatic: Yellow Turbans in Runan routed Governor Zhao Qian near Shaoling.

sentences.zh: 廣陽黃巾殺幽州刺史郭勳及太守劉衛。

translations.literal: The Guangyang Yellow Turbans killed Inspector of You Guo Xun and Administrator Liu Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Guangyang Yellow Turbans killed Guo Xun, the inspector of You, and the administrator Liu Wei.

sentences.zh: 五月，皇甫嵩、朱儁復與波才等戰於長社，大破之。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, Huangfu Song and Zhu Jun again fought Bo Cai and others at Changshe and greatly defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Huangfu Song and Zhu Jun crushed Bo Cai’s host at Changshe.

sentences.zh: 六月，南陽太守秦頡擊張曼成，斬之。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, Nanyang Governor Qin Nie attacked Zhang Mancheng and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Qin Nie, governor of Nanyang, struck Zhang Mancheng and took his head.

sentences.zh: 交阯屯兵執刺史及合浦太守來達，自稱『柱天將軍』，遣交阯刺史賈琮討平之。

translations.literal: Garrison troops in Jiaozhi seized the inspector and the governor of Hepu, Lai Da, styled themselves General Who Pillars Heaven, and he sent Inspector of Jiaozhi Jia Cong to campaign and pacify them.
translations.idiomatic: Mutinous troops in Jiaozhi captured the inspector and Hepu Governor Lai Da and proclaimed a rebel general-in-chief; Jia Cong, inspector of Jiaozhi, was dispatched and restored order.

sentences.zh: 皇甫嵩、朱儁大破汝南黃巾於西華。

translations.literal: Huangfu Song and Zhu Jun greatly defeated the Runan Yellow Turbans at Xihua.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu Song and Zhu Jun shattered the Runan Yellow Turbans at Xihua.

sentences.zh: 詔嵩討東郡，朱儁討南陽。

translations.literal: He ordered Song to campaign against Dong commandery, and Zhu Jun to campaign against Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu Song was ordered east against Dong commandery and Zhu Jun south against Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 盧植破黃巾，圍張角於廣宗。

translations.literal: Lu Zhi defeated the Yellow Turbans and besieged Zhang Jue at Guangzong.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Zhi broke the Yellow Turbans and penned Zhang Jue inside Guangzong.

sentences.zh: 宦官誣奏植，抵罪。

translations.literal: The eunuchs falsely memorialized against Lu Zhi; he was sentenced for a crime.
translations.idiomatic: The eunuchs framed Lu Zhi and had him punished and removed.

sentences.zh: 遣中郎將董卓攻張角，不克。

translations.literal: He sent General of the Household Dong Zhuo to attack Zhang Jue; he did not overcome him.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo, general of the household, was sent to relieve the siege but failed to break through.

sentences.zh: 洛陽女子生兒，兩頭共身。

translations.literal: A woman of Luoyang gave birth to a child with two heads sharing one body.
translations.idiomatic: A Luoyang woman bore a single-bodied infant with two heads.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，巴郡妖巫張脩反，寇郡縣。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, the sorcerer Zhang Xiu of Ba commandery rebelled and raided commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Zhang Xiu, a sorcerer of Ba commandery, rose in revolt and pillaged local seats.

sentences.zh: 河南尹徐灌下獄死。

translations.literal: Henan Governor Xu Guan was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Guan, governor of Henan, was thrown into prison and died there.

sentences.zh: 八月，皇甫嵩與黃巾戰於倉亭，獲其帥。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, Huangfu Song fought the Yellow Turbans at Cangting and captured their commander.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Huangfu Song defeated the Yellow Turbans at Cangting and seized their leader.

sentences.zh: 乙巳，詔皇甫嵩北討張角。

translations.literal: On yisi, he ordered Huangfu Song to campaign north against Zhang Jue.
translations.idiomatic: On yisi Huangfu Song was ordered north to finish Zhang Jue.

sentences.zh: 九月，安平王續有罪誅，國除。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, Prince Xu of Anping, guilty of an offense, was executed and the kingdom was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Liu Xu, prince of Anping, was executed for a crime and his kingdom extinguished.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，皇甫嵩與黃巾賊戰於廣宗，獲張角弟梁。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, Huangfu Song fought the Yellow Turban bandits at Guangzong and captured Zhang Jue’s younger brother Liang.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Huangfu Song defeated the Yellow Turbans at Guangzong and captured Zhang Liang, Zhang Jue’s brother.

sentences.zh: 角先死，乃戮其屍。

translations.literal: Zhang Jue had died earlier; then they mutilated his corpse.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Jue was already dead, so the victors desecrated his body.

sentences.zh: 以皇甫嵩為左車騎將軍。

translations.literal: Huangfu Song was made General of Chariots and Cavalry of the Left.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu Song was named general of chariots and cavalry of the left.

sentences.zh: 十一月，皇甫嵩又破黃巾於下曲陽，斬張角弟寶。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Huangfu Song again defeated the Yellow Turbans at Xiaquyang and beheaded Zhang Jue’s younger brother Bao.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Huangfu Song broke the Yellow Turbans at Xiaquyang and beheaded Zhang Bao.

sentences.zh: 湟中義從胡北宮伯玉與先零羌叛，以金城人邊章、韓遂為軍帥，攻殺護羌校尉伶徵、金城太守陳懿。

translations.literal: The volunteer Hu of Huangzhong, Beigong Boyu, together with the Xianling Qiang rebelled; they made the men of Jincheng Bian Zhang and Han Sui army commanders, attacked and killed Colonel Protecting the Qiang Ling Zheng and Jincheng Governor Chen Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Beigong Boyu, a Hu auxiliarist from the Huangzhong region, joined the Xianling Qiang in revolt, elevated Bian Zhang and Han Sui of Jincheng as chiefs, and killed Ling Zheng, colonel protecting the Qiang, and Chen Yi, governor of Jincheng.

sentences.zh: 癸巳，朱儁拔宛城，斬黃巾別帥孫夏。

translations.literal: On guisi, Zhu Jun took Wancheng and beheaded the Yellow Turban division commander Sun Xia.
translations.idiomatic: On guisi Zhu Jun stormed Wancheng and executed the Yellow Turban leader Sun Xia.

sentences.zh: 詔減太官珍羞，御食一肉；

translations.literal: He ordered reduction of the Grand Provisioner’s rare delicacies; for imperial meals only one meat dish.
translations.idiomatic: An edict cut back the imperial kitchen’s luxury fare so the emperor’s table might offer only a single meat course.

sentences.zh: 廄馬非郊祭之用，悉出給軍。

translations.literal: Horses of the stables that were not for use in suburban sacrifices were all issued to the army.
translations.idiomatic: Stable horses not reserved for suburban rites were turned over to the armies.

sentences.zh: 十二月己巳，大赦天下，改元中平。

translations.literal: On jisi of the twelfth month, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven and changed the reign title to Zhongping.
translations.idiomatic: On jisi in the twelfth month a general amnesty was proclaimed and the era name changed to Zhongping.

sentences.zh: 是歲，下邳王意薨，無子，國除。

translations.literal: That year, Prince Yi of Xiapi died without a son, and the kingdom was abolished.
translations.idiomatic: That year Liu Yi, prince of Xiapi, died heirless and his kingdom was abolished.

sentences.zh: 郡國生異草，備龍蛇鳥獸之形。

translations.literal: Commanderies and kingdoms produced strange grass, complete with forms of dragons, snakes, birds, and beasts.
translations.idiomatic: Odd plants sprang up in the commanderies and kingdoms shaped like dragons, snakes, birds, and beasts.

sentences.zh: 二年春正月，大疫。

translations.literal: In the second year, spring, first month, there was a great plague.
translations.idiomatic: A major epidemic struck in the first month of the second year.

sentences.zh: 琅邪王據薨。

translations.literal: Prince Ju of Langya died.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Ju, prince of Langya, died.

sentences.zh: 二月己酉，南宮大灾，火半月乃滅。

translations.literal: On jiyou of the second month, the Southern Palace had a great conflagration; only after half a month was the fire extinguished.
translations.idiomatic: On jiyou the Southern Palace burned; the flames were not brought under control for half a month.

sentences.zh: 癸亥，廣陽門外屋自壞。

translations.literal: On guihai, the buildings outside the Guangyang Gate collapsed of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On guihai structures outside the Guangyang Gate collapsed on their own.

sentences.zh: 稅天下田，畝十錢。

translations.literal: He taxed fields under Heaven, ten cash per mu.
translations.idiomatic: A levy of ten cash per mu was imposed on cultivated land empire-wide.

sentences.zh: 黑山賊張牛角等十餘輩並起，所在寇鈔。

translations.literal: Black Mountain bandit Zhang Niujiao and more than ten bands arose together, wherever they were plundering.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Niujiao and a dozen other Black Mountain chieftains rose at once and raided wherever they went.

sentences.zh: 司徒袁隗免。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Yuan Kui was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Kui was removed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 三月，廷尉崔烈為司徒。

translations.literal: In the third month, Commandant of Justice Cui Lie was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Cui Lie, commandant of justice, became minister of education.

sentences.zh: 北宮伯玉等寇三輔，遣左車騎將軍皇甫嵩討之，不克。

translations.literal: Beigong Boyu and others raided the Three Adjuncts; he sent General of Chariots and Cavalry of the Left Huangfu Song to campaign against them; he did not overcome them.
translations.idiomatic: Beigong Boyu’s rebels ravaged the Three Adjuncts; Huangfu Song, general of chariots and cavalry of the left, was sent against them but could not prevail.

sentences.zh: 夏四月庚戌，大風，雨雹。

translations.literal: On gengxu of summer, the fourth month, great wind; hail fell as rain.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu in the fourth month a violent storm brought hail.

sentences.zh: 五月，太尉鄧盛罷，太僕河內張延為太尉。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, Grand Commandant Deng Sheng was removed, and Grand Coachman Zhang Yan of Henei was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Deng Sheng left the grand commandant’s post; Zhang Yan of Henei, grand coachman, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，三輔螟。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, there were locusts in the Three Adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: Locusts swarmed the metropolitan region in the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 左車騎將軍皇甫嵩免。

translations.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry of the Left Huangfu Song was dismissed.
translations.idiomatic: Huangfu Song was stripped of his post as general of chariots and cavalry of the left.

sentences.zh: 八月，以司空張溫為車騎將軍，討北宮伯玉。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, Minister of Works Zhang Wen was made General of Chariots and Cavalry to campaign against Beigong Boyu.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month Zhang Wen, minister of works, was named general of chariots and cavalry and ordered to crush Beigong Boyu.

sentences.zh: 九月，特進楊賜為司空。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, Specially Advanced Yang Ci was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month Yang Ci, specially advanced, became minister of works.

sentences.zh: 冬十月庚寅，司空楊賜薨，光祿大夫許相為司空。

translations.literal: On gengyin of winter, the tenth month, Minister of Works Yang Ci died, and Grandee of Brilliant Candidacy Xu Xiang was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin in the tenth month Yang Ci died in office as minister of works; Xu Xiang, grandee of brilliant candidacy, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 前司徒陳耽、諫議大夫劉陶坐直言，下獄死。

translations.literal: Former Minister of Education Chen Dan and Remonstrance Grandee Liu Tao sat guilty of plain speaking; they were imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Dan, the former minister of education, and Liu Tao, a remonstrance grandee, were jailed and died for outspoken criticism.

sentences.zh: 十一月，張溫破北宮伯玉於美陽，因遣蕩寇將軍周慎追擊之，圍榆中；

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Zhang Wen defeated Beigong Boyu at Meiyang, then sent General Who Sweeps Away Bandits Zhou Shen in pursuit, besieging Yuzhong;
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Zhang Wen routed Beigong Boyu at Meiyang and sent Zhou Shen, general who sweeps away bandits, in pursuit to besiege him at Yuzhong.

sentences.zh: 又遣中郎將董卓討先零羌。

translations.literal: Again he sent General of the Household Dong Zhuo to campaign against the Xianling Qiang.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo, general of the household, was also dispatched against the Xianling Qiang.

sentences.zh: 慎、卓並不克。

translations.literal: Shen and Zhuo together did not overcome them.
translations.idiomatic: Neither Zhou Shen nor Dong Zhuo achieved victory.

sentences.zh: 鮮卑寇幽、幷二州。

translations.literal: The Xianbei raided the two provinces of You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: Xianbei raiders struck You and Bing provinces.

sentences.zh: 是歲，造萬金堂於西園。

translations.literal: That year, he built the Hall of Myriad Gold in the Western Garden.
translations.idiomatic: That year the Hall of Myriad Gold was raised in the Western Garden.

sentences.zh: 洛陽民生兒，兩頭四臂。

translations.literal: A commoner of Luoyang gave birth to a child with two heads and four arms.
translations.idiomatic: A Luoyang commoner’s child was born with two heads and four arms.

sentences.zh: 三年春二月，江夏兵趙慈反，殺南陽太守秦頡。

translations.literal: In the third year, spring, second month, the Jiangxia soldier Zhao Ci rebelled and killed Nanyang Governor Qin Nie.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the third year Zhao Ci, a mutinous soldier from Jiangxia, killed Qin Nie, governor of Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 庚戌，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On gengxu, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On gengxu a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 大尉張延罷。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Zhang Yan was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Yan was dismissed as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 車騎將軍張溫為太尉，中常侍趙忠為車騎將軍。
