translations.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry Zhang Wen was made Grand Commandant, and Household Regular Attendant Zhao Zhong was made General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Wen became grand commandant while the eunuch Zhao Zhong was oddly given the rank of general of chariots and cavalry.

sentences.zh: 復修玉堂殿，鑄銅人四，黃鐘四，及天祿、蝦蟆，又鑄四出文錢。

translations.literal: He again repaired the Jade Hall, cast four bronze men, four yellow bells, and the Tianlu and Giant Toad bronze figures, and also cast four-branched cash.
translations.idiomatic: The Jade Hall was rebuilt; four bronze figures, four yellow bells, and the Tianlu and Giant Toad bronze ornaments were cast, along with new four-snouted bronze coinage.

sentences.zh: 五月壬辰晦，日有食之。

translations.literal: On renchen, last day of the fifth month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: A solar eclipse fell on the last day of the fifth month, renchen.

sentences.zh: 六月，荊州刺史王敏討趙慈，斬之。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, Inspector of Jing Wang Min campaigned against Zhao Ci and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month Wang Min, inspector of Jing province, destroyed Zhao Ci’s band and executed him.

sentences.zh: 車騎將軍趙忠罷。

translations.literal: General of Chariots and Cavalry Zhao Zhong was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Zhong was stripped of his post as general of chariots and cavalry.

sentences.zh: 秋八月，懷陵上有雀萬數，悲鳴，因鬥相殺。

translations.literal: In autumn, the eighth month, on Huailing there were myriads of sparrows, crying mournfully, then fighting and killing one another.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month countless sparrows gathered on Huailing mound, wailing, then turned on one another until many lay dead.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，武陵蠻叛，寇郡界，郡兵討破之。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the Wuling Man rebelled, raided the commandery border, and commandery troops campaigned and defeated them.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month the tribesmen of Wuling rose up, raided the commandery frontier, and were beaten back by local forces.

sentences.zh: 前太尉張延為宦人所譖，下獄死。

translations.literal: Former Grand Commandant Zhang Yan was slandered by eunuchs, imprisoned, and died.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Yan, the former grand commandant, was framed by the eunuchs, thrown into prison, and died there.

sentences.zh: 十二月，鮮卑寇幽、幷二州。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the Xianbei raided the two provinces of You and Bing.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth month Xianbei raiders struck You and Bing provinces.

sentences.zh: 四年春正月己卯，大赦天下。

translations.literal: In the fourth year, spring, first month jimao, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On jimao in the first month of the fourth year a general amnesty was proclaimed.

sentences.zh: 二月，滎陽賊殺中牟令。

translations.literal: In the second month, bandits at Xingyang killed the magistrate of Zhongmou.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month rebels in Xingyang murdered the magistrate of Zhongmou.

sentences.zh: 己亥，南宮內殿罘罳自壞。

translations.literal: On jihai, the fusi screens of the inner hall of the Southern Palace collapsed of themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On jihai the latticed screens in the Southern Palace’s inner hall fell in without warning.

sentences.zh: 三月，河南尹何苗討滎陽賊，破之，拜苗為車騎將軍。

translations.literal: In the third month, Henan Governor He Miao campaigned against the Xingyang bandits and defeated them; He Miao was invested as General of Chariots and Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month He Miao, governor of Henan, crushed the Xingyang bandits and was rewarded with the rank of general of chariots and cavalry.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，涼州刺史耿鄙討金城賊韓遂，鄙兵大敗，遂寇漢陽，漢陽太守傅燮戰沒。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, Inspector of Liang Geng Bi campaigned against the Jincheng bandit Han Sui; Bi’s army was greatly defeated; Sui raided Hanyang, and Hanyang Governor Fu Xie died in battle.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Geng Bi, inspector of Liang, marched on Han Sui’s rebels in Jincheng and suffered a crushing defeat; Han Sui swept into Hanyang, where Governor Fu Xie fell fighting.

sentences.zh: 扶風人馬騰、漢陽人王國並叛，寇三輔。

translations.literal: The man Ma Teng of Fufeng and the man Wang Guo of Hanyang together rebelled and raided the Three Adjuncts.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Teng of Fufeng and Wang Guo of Hanyang rose together and ravaged the metropolitan region.

sentences.zh: 太尉張溫免，司徒崔烈為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Zhang Wen was dismissed, and Minister of Education Cui Lie was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Wen was removed as grand commandant; Cui Lie, minister of education, took his place.

sentences.zh: 五月，司空許相為司徒，光祿勳沛國丁宮為司空。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, Minister of Works Xu Xiang was made Minister of Education, and Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Ding Gong of Pei was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Xu Xiang moved from minister of works to minister of education, while Ding Gong of Pei became minister of works.

sentences.zh: 六月，洛陽民生男，兩頭共身。

translations.literal: In the sixth month, a commoner of Luoyang gave birth to a male child with two heads sharing one body.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth month a Luoyang commoner’s son was born with two heads on a single trunk.

sentences.zh: 漁陽人張純與同郡張舉舉兵叛，攻殺右北平太守劉政、遼東太守楊終、護烏桓校尉公綦稠等。

translations.literal: The man Zhang Chun of Yuyang together with Zhang Ju of the same commandery raised troops in rebellion, attacked and killed the governor of Youbeiping Liu Zheng, the governor of Liaodong Yang Zhong, Colonel Protecting the Wuhuan Gongqi Chou, and others.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Chun of Yuyang and his fellow townsman Zhang Ju mutinied, killing Liu Zheng of Youbeiping, Yang Zhong of Liaodong, Gongqi Chou, colonel protecting the Wuhuan, and others.

sentences.zh: 舉自稱天子，寇幽、冀二州。

translations.literal: Ju styled himself Son of Heaven and raided the two provinces of You and Ji.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Ju declared himself emperor and pillaged You and Ji provinces.

sentences.zh: 秋九月丁酉，令天下繫囚罪未決，入縑贖。

translations.literal: On dingyou of autumn, the ninth month, he ordered that prisoners in the empire whose crimes were not yet decided might enter silk brocade to redeem themselves.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou in the ninth month undecided prisoners were again allowed silk commutations.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，零陵人觀鵠自稱『平天將軍』，寇桂陽，長沙太守孫堅擊斬之。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the man Guan Hu of Lingling styled himself General Who Levels Heaven, raided Guiyang, and Changsha Governor Sun Jian attacked and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Guan Hu of Lingling, calling himself general who levels heaven, struck Guiyang; Sun Jian, governor of Changsha, ran him down and executed him.

sentences.zh: 十一月，太尉崔烈罷，大司農曹嵩為太尉。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, Grand Commandant Cui Lie was removed, and Grand Minister of Agriculture Cao Song was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Cui Lie left the grand commandant’s post; Cao Song, grand minister of agriculture, replaced him.

sentences.zh: 十二月，休屠各胡叛。

translations.literal: In the twelfth month, the Xiutuqiang Hu rebelled.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiutuqiang tribesmen rose in revolt in the twelfth month.

sentences.zh: 是歲，賣關內侯，假金印紫綬，傳世，入錢五百萬。

translations.literal: That year, he sold marquisates within the passes, lent gold seals and purple ribbons, transmissible to heirs, for five million cash entry.
translations.idiomatic: That year a marquis-within-the-passes title could be bought for five million cash, complete with the gold seal and purple ribbon and the right to pass them to an heir.

sentences.zh: 五年春正月，休屠各胡寇西河，殺郡守邢紀。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, spring, first month, the Xiutuqiang Hu raided Xihe and killed the governor Xing Ji.
translations.idiomatic: In the first month of the fifth year Xiutuqiang raiders invaded Xihe and slew Governor Xing Ji.

sentences.zh: 丁酉，大赦天下。

translations.literal: On dingyou, he issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: On dingyou a general amnesty was declared.

sentences.zh: 二月，有星孛於紫宮。

translations.literal: In the second month, there was a broom star in the Purple Palace Enclosure.
translations.idiomatic: A comet appeared in the Purple Palace asterism in the second month.

sentences.zh: 黃巾餘賊郭太等起於西河白波谷，寇太原、河東。

translations.literal: Yellow Turban remnant bandits Guo Tai and others arose in the Baibo Valley of Xihe and raided Taiyuan and Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: Guo Tai and other Yellow Turban holdouts rose from the Baibo Gorge in Xihe commandery and ravaged Taiyuan and Hedong.

sentences.zh: 三月，休屠各胡攻殺幷州刺史張懿，遂與南匈奴左部胡合，殺其單于。

translations.literal: In the third month, the Xiutuqiang Hu attacked and killed Inspector of Bing Zhang Yi, then joined with the Hu of the Southern Xiongnu’s left division and killed their Shanyu.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Xiutuqiang warriors killed Zhang Yi, inspector of Bing, then allied with the left wing of the Southern Xiongnu and murdered that faction’s Shanyu.

sentences.zh: 夏四月，汝南葛陂黃巾攻沒郡縣。

translations.literal: In summer, the fourth month, the Yellow Turbans of Gebei in Runan attacked and drowned commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month Runan’s Gebei Yellow Turbans overran county seats.

sentences.zh: 太尉曹嵩罷。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Cao Song was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Cao Song was dismissed as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 五月，永樂少府樊陵為太尉。

translations.literal: In the fifth month, Junior Prefect of Yongle Fan Ling was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth month Fan Ling, junior prefect of Yongle, became grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 六月丙寅，大風。

translations.literal: On bingyin of the sixth month, there was great wind.
translations.idiomatic: A violent windstorm struck on bingyin in the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 太尉樊陵罷。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Fan Ling was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Fan Ling was dismissed as grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 益州黃巾馬相攻殺刺史郗儉，自稱天子，又寇巴郡，殺郡守趙部，益州從事賈龍擊相，斬之。

translations.literal: The Yellow Turban Ma Xiang in Yi province attacked and killed Inspector Xi Jian, styled himself Son of Heaven, again raided Ba commandery, killed the governor Zhao Bu; Yi province clerk Jia Long attacked Xiang and beheaded him.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Xiang’s Yellow Turbans in Yi province murdered Inspector Xi Jian, proclaimed an emperor, pushed into Ba commandery, and killed Governor Zhao Bu before Jia Long, a provincial clerk, destroyed Ma Xiang and took his head.

sentences.zh: 郡國七大水。

translations.literal: Seven commanderies and kingdoms had great floods.
translations.idiomatic: Seven commanderies and kingdoms suffered serious flooding.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，射聲校尉馬日磾為太尉。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Colonel of the Archers Who Let Their Sound Ring Ma Midi was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month Ma Midi, colonel of the archers who let their sound ring, was appointed grand commandant.

sentences.zh: 八月，初置西園八校尉。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, for the first time he established the eight colonels of the Western Garden.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month the eight Western Garden colonelcies were created for the first time.

sentences.zh: 司徒許相罷，司空丁宮為司徒。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Xu Xiang was removed, and Minister of Works Ding Gong was made Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Xu Xiang left the ministry of education; Ding Gong, minister of works, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 光祿勳南陽劉弘為司空。

translations.literal: Superintendent of the Household for Imperial Proceedings Liu Hong of Nanyang was made Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Hong of Nanyang, superintendent of the household for imperial proceedings, became minister of works.

sentences.zh: 衛尉董重為票騎將軍。

translations.literal: Commandant of the Guards Dong Zhong was made General of Agile Cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhong, commandant of the guards, was named general of agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 九月，南單于叛，與白波賊寇河東。

translations.literal: In the ninth month, the Southern Shanyu rebelled and with the White Wave bandits raided Hedong.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth month the Southern Shanyu turned rebel and joined White Wave raiders in pillaging Hedong.

sentences.zh: 遣中郎將孟益率騎都尉公孫瓚討漁陽賊張純等。

translations.literal: He sent General of the Household Meng Yi leading Colonel of Cavalry Gongsun Zan to campaign against the Yuyang bandits Zhang Chun and others.
translations.idiomatic: General of the household Meng Yi was dispatched with Gongsun Zan, colonel of cavalry, to campaign against Zhang Chun’s rebels in Yuyang.

sentences.zh: 冬十月，青、徐黃巾復起，寇郡縣。

translations.literal: In winter, the tenth month, the Yellow Turbans of Qing and Xu rose again and raided commanderies and counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth month Yellow Turban remnants in Qing and Xu provinces rose again and attacked local seats.

sentences.zh: 甲子，帝自稱『無上將軍』，燿兵於平樂觀。

translations.literal: On jiazi, the emperor styled himself Supreme General and displayed troops at the Pingle Lodge observation tower.
translations.idiomatic: On jiazi the emperor took the title supreme general and reviewed troops at the Pingle observation platform.

sentences.zh: 十一月，涼州賊王國圍陳倉，右將軍皇甫嵩救之。

translations.literal: In the eleventh month, the Liang province bandit Wang Guo besieged Chencang, and General of the Right Huangfu Song relieved it.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh month Wang Guo’s rebels besieged Chencang; Huangfu Song, general of the right, marched to raise the siege.

sentences.zh: 遣下軍校尉鮑鴻討葛陂黃巾。

translations.literal: He sent Colonel of the Lower Army Bao Hong to campaign against the Gebei Yellow Turbans.
translations.idiomatic: Bao Hong, colonel of the lower army, was ordered against the Gebei Yellow Turbans.

sentences.zh: 巴郡板楯蠻叛，遣上軍別部司馬趙瑾討平之。

translations.literal: The Ban-dun Man of Ba commandery rebelled; he sent Separate Division Major of the Upper Army Zhao Jin to campaign and pacify them.
translations.idiomatic: When the Ban-dun tribes of Ba commandery revolted, Zhao Jin, separate-division major of the upper army, was sent and restored order.

sentences.zh: 公孫瓚與張純戰於石門，大破之。

translations.literal: Gongsun Zan fought Zhang Chun at Shimen and greatly defeated him.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Zan shattered Zhang Chun’s force at Shimen.

sentences.zh: 是歲，改刺史，新置牧。

translations.literal: That year, he changed regional inspectors and newly established shepherds.
translations.idiomatic: That year the office of regional inspector was reorganized into the new post of provincial shepherd.

sentences.zh: 六年春二月，左將軍皇甫嵩大破王國於陳倉。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, spring, second month, General of the Left Huangfu Song greatly defeated Wang Guo at Chencang.
translations.idiomatic: In the second month of the sixth year Huangfu Song, general of the left, shattered Wang Guo’s army at Chencang.

sentences.zh: 三月，幽州牧劉虞購斬漁陽賊張純。

translations.literal: In the third month, Shepherd of You Liu Yu offered a reward and beheaded the Yuyang bandit Zhang Chun.
translations.idiomatic: In the third month Liu Yu, shepherd of You, had Zhang Chun of Yuyang hunted down and killed for the posted reward.

sentences.zh: 下軍校尉鮑鴻下獄死。

translations.literal: Colonel of the Lower Army Bao Hong was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Bao Hong, colonel of the lower army, was jailed and died there.

sentences.zh: 夏四月丙午朔，日有食之。

translations.literal: On bingwu, new moon of summer, the fourth month, there was a solar eclipse.
translations.idiomatic: The fourth month opened on bingwu with a solar eclipse.

sentences.zh: 太尉馬日磾免，幽州牧劉虞為太尉。

translations.literal: Grand Commandant Ma Midi was dismissed, and Shepherd of You Liu Yu was made Grand Commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Ma Midi was removed as grand commandant; Liu Yu, the shepherd of You province, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 丙辰，帝崩於南宮嘉德殿，年三十四。

translations.literal: On bingchen, the emperor died in the Jiade Hall of the Southern Palace, aged thirty-four.
translations.idiomatic: On bingchen the emperor died in the Jiade Hall of the Southern Palace at the age of thirty-four.

sentences.zh: 戊午，皇子辯即皇帝位，年十七。

translations.literal: On wuwu, the imperial son Bian assumed the imperial seat, aged seventeen.
translations.idiomatic: On wuwu the imperial heir Liu Bian ascended the throne; he was seventeen.

sentences.zh: 尊皇后曰皇太后，太后臨朝。

translations.literal: He honored the empress as empress dowager; the empress dowager held court from behind the screen.
translations.idiomatic: The empress was honored as empress dowager, and she began to rule the court as regent.

sentences.zh: 大赦天下，改元為光熹。

translations.literal: He issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven and changed the reign title to Guangxi.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty followed and the era name was changed to Guangxi.

sentences.zh: 封皇弟協為渤海王。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed the emperor’s younger brother Xie as king of Bohai.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor’s younger brother Liu Xie was enfeoffed as king of Bohai.

sentences.zh: 後將軍袁隗為太傅，與大將軍何進參錄尚書事。

translations.literal: General of the Rear Yuan Kui was made Grand Tutor and jointly supervised the Masters of Writing with General-in-Chief He Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Yuan Kui, general of the rear, became grand tutor and shared oversight of the Masters of Writing with General-in-Chief He Jin.

sentences.zh: 上軍校尉蹇碩下獄死。

translations.literal: Colonel of the Upper Army Jian Shuo was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: Jian Shuo, colonel of the upper army, was thrown into prison and died.

sentences.zh: 五月辛巳，票騎將軍董重下獄死。

translations.literal: On xinsi of the fifth month, General of Agile Cavalry Dong Zhong was imprisoned and died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinsi in the fifth month Dong Zhong, general of agile cavalry, was jailed and died.

sentences.zh: 六月辛亥，孝仁皇后董氏崩。

translations.literal: On xinhai of the sixth month, Empress Dowager Xiaoren née Dong died.
translations.idiomatic: On xinhai in the sixth month Empress Dowager Dong, posthumously titled Xiaoren, died.

sentences.zh: 辛酉，葬孝靈皇帝於文陵。

translations.literal: On xinyou, Emperor Xiaoling was buried at Wen Mausoleum.
translations.idiomatic: On xinyou Emperor Ling was interred at Wen Mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 雨水。

translations.literal: Rain fell.
translations.idiomatic: Rain continued to fall.

sentences.zh: 秋七月，甘陵王忠薨。

translations.literal: In autumn, the seventh month, Prince Zhong of Ganling died.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Zhong of Ganling died in the seventh month.

sentences.zh: 庚寅，孝仁皇後歸葬河間慎陵。

translations.literal: On gengyin, Empress Dowager Xiaoren was returned for burial at Shen Mausoleum in Hejian.
translations.idiomatic: On gengyin the late Empress Dowager Dong was escorted to Hejian for burial at Shen Mausoleum.

sentences.zh: 徙渤海王協為陳留王。

translations.literal: He moved King Xie of Bohai to be king of Chenliu.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Xie was transferred from the kingship of Bohai to that of Chenliu.

sentences.zh: 司徒丁宮罷。

translations.literal: Minister of Education Ding Gong was removed.
translations.idiomatic: Ding Gong was dismissed as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 八月戊辰，中常侍張讓、段珪殺大將軍何進，於是虎賁中郎將袁術燒東西宮，攻諸宦者。

translations.literal: On wuchen of the eighth month, Household Regular Attendants Zhang Rang and Duan Gui killed General-in-Chief He Jin; thereupon Colonel of the Household Rapid as a Tiger Yuan Shu burned the eastern and western palaces and attacked the various eunuchs.
translations.idiomatic: On wuchen in the eighth month the eunuchs Zhang Rang and Duan Gui murdered He Jin; Yuan Shu, colonel of the household rapid as a tiger, then torched the inner palaces and moved against the eunuch faction.

sentences.zh: 庚午，張讓、段珪等劫少帝及陳留王幸北宮德陽殿。

translations.literal: On gengwu, Zhang Rang, Duan Gui, and others seized the young emperor and the king of Chenliu and fled to the Deyang Hall of the Northern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: On gengwu Zhang Rang and Duan Gui abducted the young emperor and the king of Chenliu and took refuge in the Deyang Hall of the Northern Palace.

sentences.zh: 何進部曲將吳匡與車騎將軍何苗戰於朱雀闕下，苗敗，斬之。

translations.literal: He Jin’s division commander Wu Kuang fought General of Chariots and Cavalry He Miao below the Vermilion Bird Gate; Miao was defeated and beheaded.
translations.idiomatic: Below the Vermilion Bird Gate Wu Kuang, a commander under He Jin, fought He Miao, general of chariots and cavalry; He Miao was routed and beheaded.

sentences.zh: 辛末，司隸校尉袁紹勒兵收偽司隸校尉樊陵、河南尹許相及諸閹人，無少長皆斬之。

translations.literal: On xinmo, Metropolitan Commandant Yuan Shao led troops to seize the false Metropolitan Commandant Fan Ling, Henan Governor Xu Xiang, and the various castrated men, without regard to age, all beheading them.
translations.idiomatic: On the day recorded as xinmo, Yuan Shao, metropolitan commandant, arrested the bogus metropolitan commandant Fan Ling, Henan Governor Xu Xiang, and every eunuch his men could find, executing them without regard to age.

sentences.zh: 讓、珪等復劫少帝、陳留王走小平津。

translations.literal: Rang, Gui, and others again seized the young emperor and the king of Chenliu and fled to Little Ping Ford.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Rang and Duan Gui again fled with the young emperor and the king of Chenliu toward Little Ping Ford.

sentences.zh: 尚書盧植追讓、珪等，斬數人，其餘投河而死。

translations.literal: Director of the Secretariat Lu Zhi pursued Rang, Gui, and others, beheaded several men; the rest threw themselves into the river and died.
translations.idiomatic: Lu Zhi, director of the secretariat, overtook the eunuchs, killed several, and drove the rest to drown themselves in the river.

sentences.zh: 帝與陳留王協夜步逐熒光行數里，得民家露車，共乘之。

translations.literal: The emperor and King Xie of Chenliu walked at night following firefly light for several li, obtained a commoner’s uncovered cart, and rode it together.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor and Liu Xie, king of Chenliu, stumbled through the dark for miles by the glimmer of fireflies, then found a peasant’s open cart and rode it together.

sentences.zh: 辛未，還宮。

translations.literal: On xinwei, they returned to the palace.
translations.idiomatic: On xinwei they were brought back to the palace.

sentences.zh: 大赦天下，改光熹為昭寧。

translations.literal: He issued a great amnesty for all under Heaven and changed Guangxi to Zhaoning.
translations.idiomatic: A general amnesty was proclaimed and the era name Guangxi was changed to Zhaoning.

sentences.zh: 幷州牧董卓殺執金吾丁原。

translations.literal: Shepherd of Bing Dong Zhuo killed Bearer of the Golden Mace Ding Yuan.
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhuo, shepherd of Bing province, murdered Ding Yuan, bearer of the golden mace.

sentences.zh: 司空劉弘免，董卓自為司空。

translations.literal: Minister of Works Liu Hong was dismissed, and Dong Zhuo personally became Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Hong was removed as minister of works; Dong Zhuo took the post himself.

sentences.zh: 九月甲戌，董卓廢帝為弘農王。

translations.literal: On jiaxu of the ninth month, Dong Zhuo deposed the emperor and made him king of Hongnong.
translations.idiomatic: On jiaxu in the ninth month Dong Zhuo deposed the emperor, reducing him to prince of Hongnong.

sentences.zh: 自六月雨，至於是月。

translations.literal: From the sixth month it rained until this month.
translations.idiomatic: Rain had fallen without cease since the sixth month.

sentences.zh: 論曰：《秦本紀》說趙高譎二世，指鹿為馬，而趙忠、張讓亦紿靈帝不得登高臨觀，故知亡敝者同其致矣。

translations.literal: The discussion says: The 《Basic Annals of Qin》 states that Zhao Gao slandered the Second Emperor, pointed to a deer and called it a horse; and Zhao Zhong and Zhang Rang likewise deceived Emperor Ling so that he could not ascend high and overlook; thus one knows that those who bring ruin and decay arrive at the same outcome.
translations.idiomatic: The historian remarks: just as the 《Basic Annals of Qin》 tells how Zhao Gao duped the Second Emperor into calling a deer a horse, Zhao Zhong and Zhang Rang tricked Emperor Ling out of climbing high towers to survey his realm; the path to ruin runs the same course.

sentences.zh: 然則靈帝之為靈也優哉！

translations.literal: In that case, for Emperor Ling to receive the posthumous title Ling was indeed apt!
translations.idiomatic: Small wonder, then, that his posthumous name should be Ling, “the numinous,” in its darker sense of dissipation!

sentences.zh: 贊曰：靈帝負乘，委體宦孽。

translations.literal: The encomium says: Emperor Ling bore the vessel while unworthy to carry it, and surrendered his person to eunuch spawn.
translations.idiomatic: The summation reads: Emperor Ling held a throne beyond his strength, the classic “carrying the vessel on his back while riding,” and gave himself over to the eunuchs’ brood.

sentences.zh: 徵亡備兆，《小雅》盡缺。

translations.literal: The omens of extinction were complete; the 《Lesser Odes》 were wholly lacking.
translations.idiomatic: Every sign of ruin stood plain, as if the moral world evoked in the 《Lesser Odes》 had been erased.

sentences.zh: 麋鹿霜露，遂棲宮衛。

translations.literal: Elk and deer amid frost and dew—thus they roosted in the palace guards.
translations.idiomatic: Deer would bed down in the frost among the palace wards where armored guards once stood.
